steps.title: Interpret the definition of tall
steps.content: A botanist classifies a plant as tall if its typical fully-grown height satisfies h > 100 cm. Any inequality that represents a tall species must have all possible heights above 100.

steps.title: Screen each choice by its lower bound
steps.content: For a compound inequality a < h < b, the smallest possible height is just above a. We need a > 100 for every height to exceed 100.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $106 \lt h \lt 158$: Lower bound is 106 > 100, so every h in this range exceeds 100. Correct
  • Choice B — $80 \lt h \lt 100$: Lower bound is 80 < 100 and upper bound is exactly 100; no value in this range exceeds 100. Incorrect
  • Choice C — $42 \lt h \lt 87$: All values are well below 100. Incorrect
  • Choice D — $17 \lt h \lt 85$: All values are well below 100. Incorrect
That matches Choice A — $106 \lt h \lt 158$.
